zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Baguley於1324年出生在英國爾巴古利時，她的父親William de Baguley III39歲，母親Clemence Dutton26歲。她於1347年與Thomas d'Anvers III在英國爾阿普爾頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1364年在英國爾黑阿德爾 赫爾姆享年40歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾格拉本哈爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Baguley于1324年出生在英国爾巴古利时，她的父亲William de Baguley III39岁，母亲Clemence Dutton26岁。她于1347年与Thomas d'Anvers III在英国爾阿普爾頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1364年在英国爾黑阿德爾 赫爾姆享年40岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾格拉本哈爾。

nameMeanings.name: Baguley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley (Cheshire). The placename is from Old English bagga denoting an unidentified animal, perhaps the badger, + lēah ‘wood, clearing’. Compare Bagley .

variants.name: Bagley
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley in Cheshire or from any of several places called Bagley, in Devon, Gloucestershire, Shropshire, Somerset, and Yorkshire. These get their names either from the Old English personal name Bacga + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ or from an unattested Old English word, bagga, for a ‘bag-shaped’ object or creature + lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Baguley was born in 1324, in Baguley, Cheshire, England, her father, William de Baguley III, was 39 and her mother, Clemence Dutton, was 26. She married Thomas d'Anvers III in 1347, in Appleton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1364, in Cheadle Hulme, Cheshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Baguley est née en 1324, à Baguley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Baguley III, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Clemence Dutton, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas d'Anvers III en 1347, à Appleton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1364, à Cheadle Hulme, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterrée à Grappenhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Baguley 1324 in Baguley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Baguley III 39 und ihre Mutter Clemence Dutton 26. Sie hat 1347 in Appleton, Cheshire, England Thomas d'Anvers III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1364 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Cheadle Hulme, Cheshire, England und wurde in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Baguleyが1324年にイギリスチェシュアイアバグリーで生まれた時，父William de Baguley IIIは39歳，母Clemence Duttonは26歳であった。1347年にイギリスチェシュアイアアップレトンでThomas d'Anvers IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1364年にイギリスチェシュアイアチェアドル ハルメで40歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアグラップエンハルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Baguley(이)가 1324년 영국 체실르 바구리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Baguley III(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Clemence Dutton(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1347년 영국 체실르 애플턴에서 Thomas d'Anvers III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1364년 영국 체실르 체아들일 훌에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 그랩홀에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas d'Anvers III em 1347, em Appleton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1364, em Cheadle Hulme, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultada em Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Baguley родилась в 1324 г. в местности багули, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Baguley III было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Clemence Dutton, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas d'Anvers III в 1347 г. в местности эпплетон, чешир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1364 г. в местности чидл хулм, чешир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и была похоронена в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Thomas d'Anvers III en 1347, en Appleton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1364, en Cheadle Hulme, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultada en Grappenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
